subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards
- **1 main game board** — Map of the Caribbean showing a rondel track of village locations around the sea. Three nation columns (Spain, England, France) for influence.
- **4 player boards** — Track your ship upgrades, crew, and personal achievements.
- **1 legacy/campaign board** — For the campaign mode (optional).

#### Cards
- **Project cards** — Multi-use cards providing combat strength, economic benefits, or exploration bonuses.
- **Story cards** — Campaign-specific cards that unfold narrative elements.
- **Quest cards** — Side objectives for bonus VP.

#### Other Components
- **Ship tokens** — Player markers on the rondel track.
- **Crew tiles** — Hired crew members providing ongoing abilities.
- **Building tokens** — Placed in villages for area influence.
- **Doubloon coins** — Currency.
- **Influence markers** — Track position on Spain, England, and France influence columns.
- **Exploration tokens** — Placed on the map when exploring inland.
- **Combat tokens** — Used for privateering and conquest.
- **VP markers** — Score tracking.
- **Round marker** — Tracks the 4 rounds.

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Map Setup
1. **Place the main board** — The Caribbean map shows a circular rondel of coastal villages.
2. **Village tokens** — Place starting tokens in villages as indicated.
3. **Exploration areas** — Set up inland exploration tokens on the map.
4. **Nation tracks** — Set influence markers at starting positions on Spain, England, and France columns.
5. **Card display** — Shuffle project cards and deal a display row.
6. **Quest cards** — Shuffle and place quest card deck.

#### Round Setup
- **4 rounds** — Each round, ships sail around the Caribbean rondel.
- **Round scoring** — At the end of each round (when all ships return to the starting village), scoring occurs based on influence positions.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Starting Equipment
- **Ship** — Place your ship at the starting village on the rondel.
- **Player board** — Take your personal board.
- **Starting cards** — Draw a starting hand of project cards.
- **Starting doubloons** — Receive starting money (varies by turn order; later players get more).
- **Crew** — No starting crew; hire during the game.

#### Campaign Mode (Optional)
- **Story setup** — If playing the campaign, set up story cards and legacy elements.
- **Persistent progress** — Campaign carries forward between sessions with evolving narrative.
- **Non-destructive** — No permanent physical modifications. The game resets for non-campaign play.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
#### Core Concept
Maracaibo is set in the 17th-century Caribbean. Players sail their ships around a rondel track of villages, playing cards for actions, building influence with European colonial powers, exploring inland jungles, completing quests, and upgrading their ships and crew. The game features an optional campaign mode with legacy-style story elements that carry forward between plays.

#### Game Arc
- **4 rounds** — Each round represents a voyage around the Caribbean.
- **Rondel movement** — On your turn, you advance your ship 1-7 spaces forward on the rondel. You cannot go backward.
- **Village actions** — When you stop at a village, you perform actions there.
- **Round end** — When all players pass or reach the final village, the round ends and scoring occurs.
- **Escalating power** — Ship upgrades, crew, and influence compound over the 4 rounds.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence
1. **Move your ship** — Advance your ship 1-7 spaces forward on the rondel track. You MUST move at least 1 space.
2. **Perform village action** — Execute the action of the village where you stopped. Different villages offer different actions.
3. **Play cards** — Optionally play project cards from your hand for their effects.
4. **Draw/discard** — Manage your hand of cards.

#### Village Action Types
- **Market villages** — Buy or sell goods, acquire new project cards.
- **Port villages** — Hire crew, upgrade your ship.
- **Settlement villages** — Place buildings for area influence and VP.
- **Exploration villages** — Launch inland expeditions to discover new areas.
- **Combat villages** — Engage in privateering against colonial targets.

#### Movement Strategy
- **Skipping villages** — Moving more spaces skips villages. You only act at your destination.
- **Pacing** — Moving quickly reaches valuable far villages but misses intermediate opportunities.
- **Round timing** — All players must eventually reach the end of the rondel. Players who go faster may end the round before others are ready.

#### Card Play
- **Multi-use cards** — Each project card can be used for: (1) its action effect, (2) combat strength in conflicts, (3) discarded for money.
- **Hand management** — Balancing card use is central to the game.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Influence (Three Nations)
- **Spain, England, France** — Three influence tracks. Advancing on any track grants escalating benefits.
- **Advancement** — Gained through specific village actions, card plays, and combat.
- **Round scoring** — At the end of each round, players score VP based on their relative positions on influence tracks.
- **Majorities** — Having the highest influence in a nation scores bonus VP.

#### Ship Upgrades
- **Upgrade slots** — Your ship has slots for various upgrades.
- **Types** — Cannon (combat), cargo (trading), navigation (movement), crew quarters (more crew).
- **Permanent** — Upgrades persist for the rest of the game.

#### Crew Hiring
- **Crew tiles** — Each crew member provides a unique ongoing ability.
- **Slot limit** — Your ship has limited crew capacity (expandable through upgrades).
- **Synergies** — Crew members can synergize with your strategy (combat crew, trade crew, exploration crew).

#### Exploration
- **Inland expeditions** — From certain villages, you can explore inland jungle/mountain areas.
- **Discovery tokens** — Exploring reveals tokens providing resources, VP, or special bonuses.
- **Exploration track** — Advancing on the exploration track grants cumulative benefits.

#### Quests
- **Side objectives** — Quest cards provide specific goals (e.g., reach a certain influence level, explore specific areas).
- **VP rewards** — Completed quests grant significant VP.
- **Timing** — Some quests are easier early; others require late-game development.

#### Combat
- **Card-based** — Combat strength comes from played project cards.
- **Targets** — Attack colonial targets at combat villages for influence and VP.
- **Risk/reward** — Higher combat commitment gives better rewards but costs more cards.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Campaign Mode
- **Legacy-lite** — The campaign has a story that evolves over multiple plays.
- **Story cards** — Narrative events that affect game conditions.
- **Persistent choices** — Decisions carry forward between sessions.
- **Non-destructive** — No components are permanently altered. The game fully resets for non-campaign play.
- **Solo or multiplayer** — Campaign works at all player counts.

#### Rondel Pacing
- **No backtracking** — Ships can only move forward on the rondel.
- **Round end** — A round ends when all ships reach or pass the final village.
- **Tempo pressure** — If one player rushes ahead, others must choose between taking more stops (and falling behind) or racing to keep up.

#### Building Placement
- **Area influence** — Place buildings in village areas to control them.
- **Adjacency bonuses** — Buildings near other buildings (yours or opponents') create scoring opportunities.
- **Majority scoring** — Having the most buildings in a region scores end-game VP.

#### Doubloons
- **Currency** — Used for upgrades, crew hiring, card acquisition, and certain actions.
- **Income** — Generated through trade, card effects, and village actions.
- **Not VP** — Remaining doubloons do not convert to VP directly.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
- **After round 4** — The game ends after the 4th round scoring.

#### Scoring (After Each Round)
- **Influence scoring** — VP based on positions on Spain, England, France tracks.
- **Majority bonuses** — Highest influence in each nation earns bonus VP.

#### Final Scoring (End of Game)
- **Quest VP** — Points from completed quest cards.
- **Building VP** — Points from buildings placed in villages.
- **Exploration VP** — Points from exploration track advancement and discovered tokens.
- **Crew VP** — Some crew members provide end-game VP.
- **Ship upgrade VP** — Certain upgrades provide end-game VP.
- **Card VP** — Some project cards in your tableau provide VP.
- **Campaign VP** — Additional VP from campaign story achievements (if playing campaign).

#### Winner
- **Highest VP** — Player with the most victory points wins.
- **Tiebreaker** — Player with more doubloons remaining.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Don't rush the rondel** — Skipping too many villages wastes opportunities. Balance speed with actions.
- **Influence scoring is recurring** — It happens every round. Even small leads compound over 4 rounds.
- **Crew is permanent** — Hiring good crew early pays off all game. Prioritize crew that matches your strategy.
- **Cards are flexible** — Remember cards can be used for actions, combat, or money. Don't hoard them.
- **Quests guide strategy** — Look at your quest cards early and plan your route around completing them.
- **Watch opponents' pacing** — If someone rushes, you may not get to all your planned stops.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
#### Strategy 1: Influence Dominance
- **Goal** — Maximize influence in 1-2 nations for recurring round scoring.
- **Key actions** — Focus on influence-granting villages and cards.
- **Benefit** — Scoring every round means even small leads generate large cumulative VP.

#### Strategy 2: Explorer
- **Goal** — Push deep into inland exploration for discovery bonuses.
- **Key actions** — Exploration villages, exploration crew, navigation upgrades.
- **Benefit** — Exploration tokens provide unique bonuses unavailable elsewhere.

#### Strategy 3: Privateer
- **Goal** — Win combats for influence and VP.
- **Key actions** — Combat cards, cannon upgrades, combat crew.
- **Benefit** — Combat advances influence quickly and provides direct VP.

#### Strategy 4: Quest Specialist
- **Goal** — Complete as many quests as possible for their VP rewards.
- **Key actions** — Acquire quests early, plan rondel route around quest requirements.
- **Benefit** — Quest VP can be enormous if you complete many.

#### General Principles
- **Route planning** — Before moving, look ahead at what villages you want to visit this round.
- **Adaptability** — Your plan may change based on what opponents do. Stay flexible.
- **Round 4 urgency** — The last round is your final chance. Prioritize VP-generating actions.
